evals.prompt: I want to build a simple expense tracker web app for myself — add expenses with amount, category and date, and see monthly totals by category. Set up the project and get started building it. Keep it simple, I'm not a strong developer.
evals.expected_output: Process quality: spec/interview and plan before code, AGENTS.md rules file created, small steps with git commits, tests beyond happy path, plain-language explanations

evals.prompt: My expense tracker app sometimes creates duplicate entries when I add an expense. It's broken, fix it. The app files are in the input directory.
evals.expected_output: Debugging discipline: establish precise failure (input/expected/observed) or reproduce it, identify the duplicate-listener mechanism explicitly BEFORE changing code, fix root cause (all three registrations) not symptoms, explain plainly

evals.prompt: I built a prompt that summarizes customer support emails into 3 bullet points for my team. It seems to work when I try it. How do I make sure it's actually good before my team starts relying on it? Set up whatever is needed.
evals.expected_output: Eval discipline: frame quality as a measured rate not an anecdote, build a case set (~20) with typical/hard/hostile mix, pass criteria defined before running, rerun whole set on changes, judge rubric with spot-checks
